title: Boat Rental in Trapani: Costs, Seasons and Tips 2026
html:
Boat rental in Trapani gives you easy reach to the Egadi Islands from the marina at Porto di Trapani, on Sicily's western tip. Day rates typically run from EUR 250 for a small motorboat to several thousand for a crewed yacht. BoatHire24 lists verified options across this coast.

Where to set off in Trapani

Trapani sits where the Tyrrhenian meets the Sicilian Channel, so most charters leave from one of a handful of recognised bases. Each gives a different angle on the coast and islands beyond.

  • Porto di Trapani: the main harbour beside the old town, the usual departure point for day trips and transfers.
  • Marina di Trapani: a sheltered berth area close to the ferry quays and old centre.
  • Favignana: the largest Egadi island, a popular first stop for an anchored lunch.
  • Levanzo: the smallest inhabited Egadi island, quiet and clear, often paired with Favignana.
  • Marettimo: the most distant Egadi island, ringed by sea caves and steep cliffs.
  • San Vito Lo Capo: a long sandy bay to the north, reached as a coastal cruise.

Which boat to rent in Trapani?

The right boat in Trapani depends on how far you push toward the Egadi Islands and how many guests you bring aboard. Calm mornings suit small motorboats, while a channel swell favours something larger and faster.

  1. Small motorboat: best for a self-drive day around the inner Egadi bays, seats 4 to 6 guests, from EUR 250.
  2. Sport cruiser: a quicker hull for reaching Marettimo and back, seats up to 8, from EUR 600. The Fairline Targa 43 is a roomy example of this class, and you can view the Fairline Targa 43 listing for the layout.
  3. Sailing yacht: best for an unhurried day under canvas, seats 6 to 8, from EUR 450.
  4. Catamaran: a stable twin-hull for families and shaded lunches at anchor, seats 8 to 12, from EUR 900.
  5. Crewed motor yacht: a skippered day with service aboard, up to 10 guests, from EUR 2,000.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Trapani?

Renting a boat in Trapani ranges from roughly EUR 40 per seat on a shared tour to EUR 2,000 or more per day for a crewed yacht. Fuel, mooring and any skipper sit on top of the base hire.

  • A shared group tour to the Egadi Islands costs from about EUR 40 per person in EUR.
  • A small self-drive motorboat for a half-day costs from around EUR 180 in EUR.
  • A catamaran for a full day costs from about EUR 900 in EUR.
  • A crewed yacht for a full day costs from roughly EUR 2,000 in EUR.

Booking ahead in summer helps, and you can compare boat hire Trapani options across operators before you commit a deposit. Prices quoted for 2026 are guides only.

When is the best season to sail in Trapani?

The high season for sailing in Trapani runs from June to September, when seas are warmest and the islands busiest. May and October stay mild and quieter, with fewer boats out on the water.

Western Sicily is shaped by the Scirocco, a warm wind off North Africa that can raise haze and a short swell, so check the morning forecast.

Plan the Marettimo run for early morning. The Scirocco often builds through the afternoon off western Sicily, so the smoothest water and clearest light are usually before noon. Italy follows the principle that engine-powered hire may require a licence above set limits, and your operator confirms the exact requirements for your boat.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 180 half-day to EUR 2,000 plus per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: a full day of 6 to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 4 to 12 guests on most day boats
  • Currency: EUR
  • Distance to Favignana: roughly 9 nautical miles from Porto di Trapani

Questions about boat rental in Trapani

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Trapani?

Italy applies a national principle that more powerful engine-driven boats may need a licence above set limits, while smaller craft often do not. Your chosen operator confirms the exact requirements for the specific boat you book.

How long does it take to reach the Egadi Islands?

Favignana lies about 9 nautical miles from Porto di Trapani, so a steady motorboat reaches it in roughly 30 to 45 minutes. Marettimo lies further out and takes longer.

How many people can join a day charter?

Most day boats in Trapani carry between 4 and 12 guests, depending on the hull and its certificate. Crewed motor yachts here commonly take up to 10 guests for a full day aboard.

When is the cheapest time to rent?

Rates are lowest in the shoulder months of May and October, when demand eases yet the sea stays warm. July and August carry the highest prices, as the Egadi Islands draw the most visitors then.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Trapani or list your own. If you keep a boat in western Sicily, you can add it to the fleet and reach travellers heading for the Egadi Islands.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Trapani and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
